name: European TTF Natural Gas
summary: €44.46/MWh — eased materially from €49.95 over the past two days as ceasefire holds and Ras Laffan restart timeline solidifies (delayed to August 2026, not May). Still +43% above pre-war €31.20. Decoupling from US gas confirmed: Henry Hub at $2.65 (-0.6%). EU storage at 30% (five-year seasonal low) — summer refill vulnerability window (May-Oct) remains the structural risk regardless of ceasefire. Cheniere LNG (US export) barely moved (-0.1%) as TTF spread narrows. TTF is now keyed to Hormuz status (operational), not headline announcements (political) — confirmed by the April 9 round-trip from €42.50 back to €49.95. Current downward move reflects physical LNG restoration expectations: ceasefire eased LNG supply fears but Qatar Ras Laffan restart is now August, not May. For ECB: benign core 2.6% in US CPI + TTF easing = rate hike pressure reducing, but stagflation trap persists through summer refill.

signals.content: TTF at 54.52 euros/MWh — doubled from pre-crisis ~25 euros but still well below 2022 peak of ~350. Primary drivers: Ras Laffan repair timeline slipping (Reuters reports Q3 vs Q2), Hormuz toll booth reducing LNG tanker transits, and Houthi entry raising Red Sea risk for LNG carriers. European gas storage at ~35% (adequate for spring but problematic if refill is disrupted for summer). (Sources: OilPriceAPI, ICE Endex, Reuters)

TTF surged 70% in March (€38→€54.52) — worst monthly gain since Sep 2021. European gas storage at 30% capacity after harsh winter (historically low). Four countries enacted emergency energy measures: Spain cut energy VAT from 21% to 10%, Italy allocated €100M for industrial support, Slovakia and Slovenia introduced pump rationing, France removed winter diesel requirements. ECB postponed planned rate cut March 19, raised inflation forecast, cut GDP growth projection — stagflation trap confirmed alongside Fed and RBI. Atlantic Council published 'How the Iran war could trigger a European energy crisis.' Bloomberg: 'Energy Price Shock Is Getting Very Real in Europe.' (Sources: Euronews, IRU, Atlantic Council, Bloomberg, OilPriceAPI)

TTF pulled back slightly to €54.18/MWh (-1.9%) tracking Brent de-escalation hopes after diplomacy signals. Slight pullback but underlying drivers unchanged: Ras Laffan structural damage (5-year repair), Hormuz toll system reducing LNG tanker transits, European storage at 30% (critically low heading into refill season). If blockade persists through summer, €80-100/MWh still realistic. (Sources: OilPriceAPI, ICE Endex)

Eurostat flash estimate: Eurozone inflation surged to 2.5% (from 1.9% in Feb), with energy component swinging from -3.1% to +4.9% — an 8 percentage point reversal in one month, the most dramatic since 2022 Russia-Ukraine shock. Shell warned Europe could face 'shortage of fuel as early as April.' TTF at €53.73/MWh (slightly cooled from €55.80 but +72% from pre-crisis). EU gas storage at ~30% — lowest seasonal level since before 2022. Chemical and steel manufacturers imposing surcharges up to 30%. 'Permanent deindustrialization' language appearing in European business media. BASF raised prices 30%. Huntsman added €200/mt gas surcharge. After IEA's 400M barrel SPR release, only Japan (124 days) meets 90-day reserve requirement; Germany 76 days, France 70, UK 39, Australia 27. (Sources: Eurostat, Shell, CNBC, Euronews, Atlantic Council, IEA)

TTF at EUR 47.51/MWh, easing on de-escalation hopes (-6.4% daily). Goldman forecasts EUR 55 for April. EU gas storage at 30% — a five-year seasonal low. To meet winter targets, EU needs to inject 60 bcm by December, which is structurally impossible without Hormuz LNG supplies resuming. Shell's April fuel shortage warning has arrived: 'we are now IN the window.' Slovenia rationing. Spain spending EUR 5B on emergency energy measures. 1.2M bpd of NW European refining capacity offline for scheduled maintenance, compounding the crisis at the worst possible time. CONFIRMED. (Sources: TradingEconomics, ICE Endex, Goldman, Shell)

Heating oil +3.7% vs gasoline -7.3% SPLIT signal — the distillate crisis is structural even if headline crude normalizes. Approximately 10% of global gasoil and ~20% of jet fuel transits Hormuz directly (Kpler data). Europe's energy crisis is deepening independent of headline oil prices because middle distillates (diesel, heating oil, jet fuel) face specific supply constraints that crude oil easing does not resolve. This divergence signals a structural refining/supply chain problem, not just a commodity price issue. CONFIRMED. (Sources: Kpler, EIA)

TTF eased to €49.95/MWh (from €55.80 peak March 30) — still +60% from pre-crisis. Easing driven by de-escalation hopes on April 1 (Pezeshkian peace signal) but structural supply deficit unchanged — Qatar LNG remains offline, Ras Laffan 5-year repair. ECB projects inflation surging to 3.1% Q2 driven by energy component. EU gas storage at ~30% — lowest seasonal level. Shell: fuel shortages 'as early as April.' ECB rate hike probability at 84%. The TTF-to-ECB feedback loop at maximum weight (10.0 both directions) — gas prices drive inflation expectations which drive ECB hawkishness which constrains fiscal response. (Sources: OilPriceAPI, CNBC, Euronews, ECB)

TTF at EUR 49.98/MWh (April 5). Down -0.6% from April 4 EUR 50.43. Still 60%+ above pre-crisis EUR 31. Was +50% weekly jump per Trading News. Qatar LNG offline. ECB raised inflation forecast to 2.6%. TTF gas is the transmission mechanism for ECB policy — if it re-tests EUR 55+, rate hike becomes near-certain. (Sources: OilPriceAPI, ICE Endex, Trading News)

TTF at €49.95/MWh, estimated +3-5% 1D. March range €43.30-61.85 (avg €53.08). Still 60%+ above pre-crisis €31.20. Qatar LNG shutdown + Hormuz shipping risk maintaining elevated prices. ECB revised 2026 inflation forecast UP to 2.6% due to energy costs, growth forecast DOWN to 0.9%. Eurozone March inflation jumped to 2.5% with energy component +4.9%. Rate hike speculation emerging for first time since 2023 — ECB in same stagflation trap as Fed. TTF directly feeds European fertilizer production costs via Haber-Bosch process (BASF +10.4% 1M). (Sources: ICE Endex, oilpriceapi.com, ECB, Eurostat)

TTF holding EUR 49.95/MWh — at the EUR 50 ceiling. Range 48.74-51.74. Qatar LNG offline persists. South Pars #2 strike re-prices Qatar exposure: South Pars/North Dome shared geological structure means Qatar's $200B+ LNG expansion is now exposed to escalation precedent. While Israel struck the Iranian petrochemical plant (NOT the gas reservoir), the precedent is alarming for any Qatar reservoir hit. Cheniere LNG (US export beneficiary) +13.8% 1M / +45.3% 3M reflects the structural Qatari/Iranian gap. European Easter Monday closure of equity markets reduced market noise — TTF will move materially Wednesday once deadline outcome is known.

EVENING UPDATE: TTF EUR 52.21/MWh (+4.2% 1D, +47% YoY). Qatar LNG disruption + deadline anxiety. EU storage at 28% — five-year seasonal low. BASF price hikes +30% from war-driven feedstock costs, Deutsche Bank upgraded to Buy. TTF directly feeding fertilizer production costs (urea via Haber-Bosch process). Rate hike speculation emerging in Europe first time since 2023. (Sources: ICE Endex, TradingEconomics, Deutsche Bank)

EUROPEAN TTF GAS LAGGING THE CEASEFIRE RELIEF — €49.95-50.40/MWh [REPORTED]. Pre-ceasefire TTF was at €52.21/MWh (April 7 evening brief). Wednesday morning data shows €49.95-50.40/MWh — minor decline so far. The market had a brief decline earlier this week on ceasefire optimism, then reversed when Trump escalated. Now the actual ceasefire is in place but European TTF has not yet repriced fully — likely due to (a) European trading day not yet open, (b) cautious wait for confirmed Qatar Ras Laffan restart timeline, (c) reinsurance restoration lag preventing immediate Western LNG carrier transits via Hormuz. EU storage at 28% (5-year seasonal low) means European market is still vulnerable. If ceasefire holds and Ras Laffan restart begins, TTF could fall toward €40-45 within 2 weeks. Wed European session is the test.

TTF ROUND-TRIP CONFIRMS HORMUZ KEY [CONFIRMED]. European TTF gas crashed -20% to €42.50/MWh on initial ceasefire reaction (hopes of Qatar Ras Laffan restart + Hormuz reopening lifting LNG carrier transit risk), then REBOUNDED back near €49.95/MWh as Iran re-closed the Strait of Hormuz citing Israeli strikes on Lebanon. The intraday round-trip is the cleanest European market signal that physical gas traders did NOT believe the ceasefire framework would deliver Qatar LNG flows. TTF is now keyed to Hormuz status (operational), not headline ceasefire announcements (political). EU storage at 28% — 5-year seasonal low — means structural vulnerability persists. Cheniere LNG -3.2% as US LNG export economics weakened on TTF round-trip.

Day 44. TTF EASED TO €44.46/MWh [CONFIRMED]. European TTF gas down from €49.95 to €44.46/MWh (approximately -11% over two sessions), confirming the April 9 round-trip was a temporary rebound and physical market is now pricing ceasefire durability with Ras Laffan restart expectation. Still +43% above pre-war €31.20. Decoupling from US gas confirmed: Henry Hub at $2.65 (-0.6%) — US domestic disconnected from global crisis. Heating oil -5.0% on seasonal demand + nat gas substitution reinforces the distillate-substitution pathway. EU storage at 30% — five-year seasonal low — means summer refill vulnerability window (May-October) remains the structural risk regardless of ceasefire durability. IMPORTANT DELAY: Ras Laffan recovery timeline now August 2026 (not May 2026 as previously reported). This gives TTF a clear path lower if the ceasefire holds through summer but creates persistent vulnerability if the August restart slips further. Cheniere LNG (US export) barely moved (-0.1%) as the TTF spread narrows. For ECB: US CPI core benign at 2.6% + TTF easing = rate hike pressure reducing; stagflation trap persists but with reduced intensity through summer. ECB rate hike probability should compress materially from 76-84% over the next 1-2 weeks if TTF continues easing. TTF trigger 'reaches €100/MWh' remains far away at €44.46 — 2.25x needed. (Sources: oilpriceapi.com, ICE Endex, TradingEconomics, markets.md G)

price_snapshots.note: EUR 47.51/MWh (-6.4% 1D) — exceeds 3% threshold, de-escalation driven pullback

edges.context: Qatar is the world's largest LNG exporter. Ras Laffan damage and Hormuz disruption directly reduce European gas supply.
TTF is the benchmark for European LNG contracts. TTF spike = European industry cost spike = inflation.
Natural gas is the primary feedstock for urea/fertilizer production. TTF spike → fertilizer production costs rise → food security cascade.

TTF directly feeds ECB inflation — energy component swung from -3.1% to +4.9% in one month (8 pp reversal). Gas costs cascading through European industrial economy. ECB rate hike at 76% probability for June.

trigger_points.mechanism: Would trigger European emergency energy protocols, industrial demand destruction, and fertilizer production shutdowns. Currently at 54.18 — needs to nearly double.
